title: use containers/storage/pkg/fileutils/(Exists,Lexists)
body:
use a faster alternative to os.Stat and os.Lstat to check whether a path is present

comments.body: Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.
Lot of complaining from CI about os.IsNotExist. Changes LGTM otherwise.
we need https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/5469 first
Is this something we should meantion to RHIVOS as a potentially speedup of podman container start?

> Is this something we should meantion to RHIVOS as a potentially speedup of podman container start?

I doubt that this has a measurable impact over the whole container start duration.
Cockpit tests failed for commit f86b67a6f1299c4bbf8d42422d1ef1753993fac0. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.
setting `bloat_approved` as the additional size depends on the vendoring
Cockpit tests failed for commit 8567fac48ddf5ef8203f9ea209c91eb032d29039. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.
Cockpit tests failed for commit 777e6cc1a9857d634b833a47b8e6c3dc8f9f6f98. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.
add to adapt a test because of https://github.com/containers/image/commit/b6afa8ca7b324aca8fd5a7b5b206fc05c0c04874
Cockpit tests failed for commit fda9f2318056d604c6121812351b27825edcfbbe. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.
Cockpit tests failed for commit 8cdab14aedbcb8960e88db16b958740d662dde42. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.

This [fails](https://artifacts.dev.testing-farm.io/0693ad75-cac4-4e85-8b48-bea9c097853a/) with
```
    self.assertEqual(self.execute(auth, "podman inspect --format '{{.Config.Healthcheck}}' healthy").strip(),
AssertionError: '{[true] 5s 0s 5m25s 35s 2}' != '{[true] 5s 5m25s 35s 2}'
- {[true] 5s 0s 5m25s 35s 2}
?           ---
+ {[true] 5s 5m25s 35s 2}
```

I.e. it changes the format of the `.Config.Healthcheck` API field. 5s is the start period, 5m25s is the interval, 35s the timeout, 2 the number of retries. I'm not sure what the "0s" was, but that field dropped. Is that intentional? Then I'll adjust cockpit-podman's tests. Or accidental?

I'm fairly sure that this is triggered by the changes in this PR, as e.g. #22372 succeeded, and *that* healthcheck test also passed in #22322 (that just failed something different).

The change is intentional and caused by the re-vendoring of c/image.

It is caused by: https://github.com/containers/image/commit/b6afa8ca7b324aca8fd5a7b5b206fc05c0c04874
Cockpit tests failed for commit d4ddd190c26e7fda9e9dfb201a2ad3cda826f0f1. @martinpitt, @jelly, @mvollmer please check.
tests are passing
OK, I sent https://github.com/cockpit-project/cockpit-podman/pull/1666 to accept either output format. This is an API break, let's hope it doesn't affect actual users..
That cockpit-podman PR landed, so please retry the cockpit-devdeps tests or force-push. (Or decide that it's an API break and not actually land it, of course :grin)

> OK, I sent [cockpit-project/cockpit-podman#1666](https://github.com/cockpit-project/cockpit-podman/pull/1666) to accept either output format. This is an API break, let's hope it doesn't affect actual users..

This is not an API break! This is adding a new field to a structure, if client code cannot handle that then it's parsing is broken. We add new fields all the time. If you want specific fields only then you should query only the fields you needs to check, i.e. `--format "{{.Config.Healthcheck.Test}}\n{{.Config.Healthcheck.Test.Interval}} ..."`
Ack -- the PR landed now, so a `/packit retest-failed` (or next force-push) should go green.
restarted the tests
Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.
